model_structure: Review of trial-based and model-based CEAs
complications_or_outcomes: ICERs, CHEQUE quality assessment, GRADE certainty
sensitivity_or_uncertainty: Quality and certainty assessment across included studies
method_strength: High-level map of pharmacologic obesity CEA evidence and bias/certainty standards
method_gap_relative_to_current_project: Not a de novo model; does not itself solve this model's price or retained-effect questions
transferable_to_current_project: Use as context that economic evidence is heterogeneous and certainty is often limited.

model_structure: Core Obesity Model v26 Markov state-transition model
complications_or_outcomes: BMI, systolic blood pressure, glycemic status, obesity risk factors
method_strength: Richer obesity-risk-factor model using a recognized Core Obesity Model platform
method_gap_relative_to_current_project: Industry-associated and jurisdiction-specific; less transparent to reproduce from public tables alone
transferable_to_current_project: Shows that obesity CEAs commonly use multi-risk-factor Markov structures and PSA/DSA.

model_structure: Individual patient simulation with R implementation
complications_or_outcomes: Type 2 diabetes progression, OSA remission, HbA1c, diet-and-exercise discontinuation
method_gap_relative_to_current_project: ICERs, QALYs, costs, clinical outcomes, convergence outputs
transferable_to_current_project: Strong example of method thickening through OSA/T2D progression and transparent implementation details
column14: Targets tirzepatide and UK assumptions, not semaglutide Canadian public payer setting
column15: Supports discussion that full obesity models increasingly include OSA and richer T2D progression.

data_source: SELECT cardiovascular outcomes trial; US healthcare sector perspective
complications_or_outcomes: CV events, T2D progression, CKD, CV death
method_strength: Uses trial-derived cardiovascular outcome data and multiple complications
method_gap_relative_to_current_project: Population has established CVD without diabetes; not directly comparable to general obesity-management cohort
transferable_to_current_project: Supports the claim that omission of CV and CKD pathways can materially change value estimates.

data_source: SELECT-like semaglutide CVD evidence; Canadian payer perspective
model_structure: Decision analytic Markov model with monthly cycles
complications_or_outcomes: Ischemic stroke, heart failure hospitalization/urgent visit, myocardial infarction, death
method_strength: Canadian cardiovascular semaglutide model with payer-relevant threshold analysis
method_gap_relative_to_current_project: CVD population and indication differ from the current obesity-management reference model
transferable_to_current_project: Useful Canadian precedent for price threshold and CV-pathway sensitivity framing.

data_source: SURMOUNT-5 head-to-head phase-3 trial; US societal perspective
complications_or_outcomes: CVD, OSA and other obesity-related complications via cardiometabolic parameters
figures_or_outputs: ICER, incremental net health benefit, costs, QALYs
method_strength: Richer comparative pharmacoeconomic simulation with societal outcomes
method_gap_relative_to_current_project: Different intervention comparison and US societal perspective; not directly transferable
transferable_to_current_project: Shows current hotspot studies use patient-level simulation and multiple obesity-related complications.

data_source: STEP trials; CADTH; Statistics Canada; Canadian public sources
complications_or_outcomes: No diabetes, type 2 diabetes, death; price and benefit frontier extensions
column17: Markov structure, CE plane, CEAC, tornado, price threshold, CADTH benchmark, value frontier and driver figure
column18: High transparency, public-source reproducibility, explicit benchmark-boundary statement, executable code
column19: Single-complication structure remains thinner than Core Obesity Model or SELECT-style CV models
column20: Use threshold and driver analyses to show how much omitted non-diabetes benefit would be required to change conclusions.
